data.title: SIX CEO Remains Optimistic About IPO Prospects Despite Iran Conflict
data.description: The CEO of SIX, which operates the Swiss and Spanish stock exchanges, remains hopeful that this year will see an improvement in initial public offerings compared to last year. Bloomberg posted on X, however, that the ongoing conflict in Iran is prompting companies to reconsider their IPO plans. Despite the geopolitical tensions, the exchange operator's leader maintains a positive outlook for the market's potential recovery.

data.title: Coinbase and Chainlink expand DeFi application scenarios by putting order books and derivatives data on-chain.
data.description: Coinbase announced that, for the first time, it has directly pushed its exchange market data onto the blockchain via Chainlink DataLink, including order books and derivatives data such as futures and perpetual contracts. This integration covers data streams from Coinbase International Exchange and Coinbase Derivatives Exchange, enabling on-chain protocols and applications to access and verify various market information, including perpetual contracts, stocks, and commodities, in real time. Coinbase stated that this move will not only help it commercialize its core market data resources and advance its "all-in-one exchange" and comprehensive prime brokerage strategy, but will also provide institutional-grade benchmark data for DeFi and traditional financial developers, supporting application innovations such as derivatives and tokenized assets. Chainlink pointed out that this collaboration is expected to provide a more secure and reliable data infrastructure for on-chain finance, driving the development of the next-generation financial system. (The Block)

data.title: New York Lawmakers Propose Tax Increase on Startup Investment Gains
data.description: New York lawmakers have introduced a proposal to increase taxes on investment gains from startups, sparking opposition from the tech industry. Bloomberg posted on X that the industry argues the tax hike could deter venture capital investments in the state, potentially impacting the growth of new businesses. The proposal has ignited a debate over its potential effects on New York's economic landscape, with tech leaders expressing concerns about the long-term implications for innovation and entrepreneurship.

data.title: Iran Calls for Legally Binding Mechanism to Prevent Future Wars, Iranian Media Reports
data.description: Iran has called for the establishment of a legally binding mechanism to ensure that wars do not recur, according to Iranian media reports. This move underscores Iran's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region. The proposal highlights the importance of international cooperation in preventing conflicts and fostering a secure global environment.

data.title: AI Crypto Sector Rises Over 10% Amid Market Shift
data.description: The AI crypto sector experienced a significant increase of 10.67% on March 25, 2026, as its combined market capitalization grew from $17.60 billion to $19.48 billion. According to NS3.AI, this surge was accompanied by a 19.34% rise in trading volume, reaching $4.95 billion. The upward movement is attributed to a broader risk-on shift in the market following U.S. President Donald Trump's five-day suspension of strikes against Iran.

data.title: U.S. Interior Secretary Claims $100 Million in Gold Brought Back from Venezuela
data.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum announced that during his recent visit to Venezuela, he returned with $100 million worth of physical gold. According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing discussions between the U.S. and Venezuela regarding economic and diplomatic relations. The acquisition of gold is seen as a strategic move to bolster the U.S. reserves. The visit highlights the complex geopolitical dynamics between the two nations, as they navigate issues related to trade and resource management. The implications of this gold acquisition on the broader economic landscape remain to be seen.

data.title: Philippine President Marcos Jr. Signs Law to Adjust Excise Tax on Petroleum Amid Fuel Price Surge
data.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has enacted legislation allowing him to suspend or lower the excise tax on petroleum products. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes as the ongoing conflict in Iran continues to drive fuel prices higher. The law aims to provide relief to consumers facing increased costs due to the geopolitical situation affecting global oil markets. The move is part of broader efforts to stabilize the economy and mitigate the impact of external factors on domestic fuel prices.

data.title: U.S. Officials Yet to Confirm Attack on USS Abraham Lincoln, Reports The Times
data.description: U.S. officials have not yet confirmed whether the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier has been attacked, according to The Times. The report highlights ongoing uncertainties surrounding the situation, with no official statements released by the U.S. government at this time. The lack of confirmation has left many speculating about the potential implications for U.S. military operations and regional stability. Further updates are anticipated as more information becomes available.

data.title: Germany Ready to Join International Stabilization Efforts After Iran Ceasefire, Says Chancellor Merz
data.description: Germany is prepared to participate in international efforts to stabilize the region following a ceasefire in the Iran conflict, according to German Chancellor Friedrich Merz. According to Jin10, Chancellor Merz emphasized Germany's commitment to contributing to peace and stability in the Middle East. The announcement comes amid ongoing diplomatic discussions aimed at resolving tensions in the region. Chancellor Merz highlighted the importance of collaborative international efforts to ensure lasting peace and security. Germany's involvement is expected to focus on diplomatic and humanitarian initiatives, aligning with broader international strategies to address the aftermath of the conflict. The German government is coordinating with other nations to develop a comprehensive plan for stabilization, reflecting its dedication to global peacekeeping efforts.

data.title: Energy Fuels Achieves Milestone in Rare Earth Element Production
data.description: Energy Fuels has successfully produced a heavy rare earth element at its Utah facility, marking a significant step in establishing a domestic supply of essential minerals. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of these minerals in electronics and defense technology. This development is part of broader efforts to reduce reliance on foreign sources for critical materials.

data.title: CIMG released its Q1 financial report: Bitcoin holdings totaled 730 coins, with a book value of nearly $64 million.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Nasdaq-listed CIMG released its Q1 2026 financial results, disclosing that as of December 31, 2025, it held 730 Bitcoins with a book value of $63,978,821. Furthermore, the company's total revenue for the three months ending December 31, 2025, was $15,768,796, compared to $22,853 in the same period last year. (Prnewswire)

data.title: Russia's Atomic Energy Chief Evacuates 163 from Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Plant
data.description: Russia's Atomic Energy Corporation has evacuated 163 individuals from the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant in Iran, according to Russian media reports. The evacuation was led by the corporation's head, Alexey Likhachev, on Wednesday. According to Jin10, the move comes amid heightened tensions in the region, although specific reasons for the evacuation were not disclosed. The Bushehr plant, a key facility in Iran's nuclear program, has been a focal point of international attention. The evacuation underscores ongoing geopolitical complexities surrounding nuclear energy and regional security.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Harvey Secures $200 Million in Funding, Valued at $11 Billion
data.description: Harvey, a startup specializing in artificial intelligence solutions for law firms, has successfully raised $200 million in its latest funding round, bringing the company's valuation to $11 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant investment in the AI sector. This funding round underscores the growing interest and confidence in AI-driven legal technology, as Harvey continues to expand its offerings and market presence.

data.title: Gold and silver rose, while cryptocurrency volatility fell, with both the BVIX and EVIX dropping by more than 3%.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,566.21 per ounce, a daily increase of 2.05%. Silver prices rose to $72.860 per ounce, a daily increase of 2.37%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 51.40, a daily decrease of 3.91%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 73.20, a daily decrease of 3.16%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate rose 0.18% to 6.90488. The USD/JPY exchange rate rose 0.25% to 159.035. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 rose 0.10% to 5,647.68 points; the UK FTSE 100 rose 0.83% to 10,089.53 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.67% to 23,012.35 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil fell 0.24% to $88.42 per barrel, while Brent crude oil fell 0.61% to $99.17 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Advent Plans $1 Billion Investment in Defense Amid Rising Global Tensions
data.description: Advent is reportedly preparing to invest up to $1 billion in the defense sector, according to a source familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's strategic move as global tensions continue to escalate. The investment reflects Advent's focus on capitalizing on the growing demand for defense capabilities worldwide. This decision comes amid increasing geopolitical uncertainties, prompting companies to bolster their defense-related investments. Advent's substantial financial commitment underscores the sector's significance in the current global landscape.

data.title: CoinDesk 20 Index Rises 2.9% with Stellar Leading Gains
data.description: The CoinDesk 20 index experienced a notable increase, closing at 2063.87, marking a 2.9% rise or an addition of 58.76 points from the previous day's close. According to NS3.AI, all 20 constituents of the index showed positive movement, with Stellar leading the gains with a 6.0% increase.

data.title: Germany's Chancellor Merz: Ending Iran War Key to Tackling High Prices
data.description: Germany's Chancellor Merz has stated that the most effective solution to combat high prices is to bring an end to the war in Iran. According to Jin10, Merz emphasized that the federal budget cannot cover all the additional costs arising from the conflict. The ongoing situation has placed a significant financial burden on the government, highlighting the need for a resolution to stabilize the economy. Merz's comments reflect growing concerns over the economic impact of geopolitical tensions and the importance of diplomatic efforts to address these challenges.

data.title: U.S. White House Trade Advisor Navarro: Powell's Continued Tenure at the Fed 'Absolutely Harmful'
data.description: U.S. White House Trade Advisor Peter Navarro has expressed strong opposition to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell remaining in his position after his current term ends. According to Jin10, Navarro stated that Powell's continued leadership at the Federal Reserve would be 'absolutely harmful.' This comment reflects ongoing tensions between the White House and the Federal Reserve regarding monetary policy and economic management. Navarro's remarks highlight the administration's dissatisfaction with Powell's handling of interest rates and other economic measures. The statement adds to the broader debate over the future direction of U.S. monetary policy and the potential impact on the economy.

data.title: Germany Proposes Joint Ventures for Foreign Companies in Europe
data.description: Germany's Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil has proposed that foreign companies be required to establish joint ventures with local firms to operate within Europe. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Klingbeil's call for increased scrutiny over cross-border takeovers. This move aims to bolster local partnerships and ensure greater oversight of foreign investments in the region. The proposal reflects Germany's strategic approach to safeguarding its economic interests while fostering collaboration between domestic and international businesses.

data.title: AI Companies Boost New York Office Leasing to Decade High
data.description: AI companies are increasingly securing office space in New York, contributing to the city's strongest year for office leasing in over ten years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the trend as AI firms expand their operations, requiring more physical space for human employees. This surge in demand is revitalizing the office leasing market, which had been sluggish in previous years. The influx of AI companies is seen as a significant factor in driving the city's commercial real estate sector forward, marking a notable shift in the urban business landscape.
